10 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

(Without Wanting to Throw Your Laptop Out the Window)
I asked ChatGPT to build me a button. It gave me 47 lines of spaghetti, 3 unused imports, and a component that re-renders 12 times per click. Here’s everything I learned about not doing that.
The Hook: Why This Post Exists
Let me paint you a picture.
It’s 2 AM. You’ve got a demo tomorrow. You’re staring at a blinking cursor, coffee gone cold, and you think: “I’ll just ask AI to write this component real quick.”
You type: “Write a React login form.”
AI responds instantly. You copy-paste. It looks… fine? You run it. It works? Kind of? You ship it.
Three weeks later, your teammate opens the file and sends you a Slack message that just says: “Bro.”
What follows is a 47-message thread about why the component:

• Has 8 props that no one understands

• Re-renders the entire page on every keystroke

• Uses useEffect for literally everything (we talked about this, people)

• Has a CSS file named styles.module.css.final.v2.actual.css
